name: Levant
climate: Mediterranean along the coast with hot dry summers and mild wet winters; semi-arid to arid inland; desert in eastern Syria and southern Jordan

neighbors.name: Mediterranean
neighbors.relevance: Western coastline; Eastern Mediterranean gas fields and shipping lanes

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Jordan and Israel have Red Sea ports at Aqaba and Eilat

neighbors.name: Suez Canal
neighbors.relevance: Adjacent across Sinai; Levant stability affects Suez Canal security

neighbors.name: Persian Gulf
neighbors.relevance: Iraqi border connects Levant to Gulf oil economy; Iranian influence corridor

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: Major source of investment, remittances, and reconstruction funding for Levant states

links.description: Eastern Mediterranean gas discoveries (Leviathan, Tamar) link Levant to EU energy markets
Levant trade depends on Suez Canal access; Sinai instability threatens both
Aqaba and Eilat ports provide Levant access to Indian Ocean trade routes
GCC sovereign wealth funds invest in Levant reconstruction and infrastructure
EU is the largest trade partner and aid donor for Levant economies
Iran-Israel rivalry plays out across the Levant via proxy forces in Syria and Lebanon
